page: Google Cloud Platform n4-standard-48 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance featuring 48 vCPUs and 192 GB of system memory. Powered by an Intel x86_64 processor with 24 physical cores and hyper-threading enabled, this instance allocates dedicated CPU resources. The processor features a 260 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets, including Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512). It does not include local storage or bundled GPU accelerators.

Benchmark data shows strong performance in memory bandwidth, with read-only small block cached speeds reaching 3417.19 GB/sec and large block uncached write speeds at 114.19 GB/sec. Multi-core performance is robust, as evidenced by a Geekbench multi-core score of 17663.0 and a Passmark CPU score of 58480.28. The hardware excels in specialized tasks, demonstrating strong capabilities in database operations, static web serving throughput at 67.45 GB/sec, and prompt processing for small to medium large language models.

Given its balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and dedicated compute resources, the n4-standard-48 offers qualitative resource density for enterprise workloads. The absence of local storage and GPUs makes it a cost-efficient choice for workloads reliant on network-attached storage and CPU-bound processing. It is highly suited for database operations, static web serving, software compilation, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: The Google Cloud Platform n4-standard-48 is an x86_64 general-purpose server instance configured with 48 dedicated vCPUs and 192 GB of RAM. Powered by an Intel processor with 24 physical cores and a 260 MB L3 cache, it supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX. This instance does not include bundled GPUs or local storage. Performance benchmarks indicate strong memory bandwidth, with uncached read speeds of 259.28 GB/sec, and high multi-core efficiency, reflected in a Passmark CPU score of 58480.28. It delivers strong results in database operations, static web serving, and prompt processing for small to medium LLMs. The n4-standard-48 is well-suited for enterprise database hosting, web serving, software compilation, and CPU-based machine learning workloads.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n4-standard-48 is an x86_64 general-purpose instance with 48 dedicated Intel vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and strong memory bandwidth, designed for database hosting and web serving.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n4-standard-48 is a general-purpose instance featuring 48 dedicated Intel vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and advanced AMX instruction support.
tagline: A dedicated general-purpose instance featuring high memory bandwidth and advanced matrix extensions for demanding enterprise database and web workloads.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated Intel vCPUs and 192 GB of RAM
Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction set support
Strong memory bandwidth and multi-core benchmark performance
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Optimized for database operations, web serving, and CPU-based inference
